bills.title: RELATING TO TRANSPORTATION.
bills.raw_subject:
Conforms ROH provisions to House Bill 2021, HD2, SD2, CD1, Relating to Transportation. Adds definitions for “Electric Bicycle,” “Class 1 Electric Bicycle,” Class 2 Electric Bicycle,” Class 3 Electric Bicycle, “Electric Micro-mobility Device, “High-speed Electric Device,” and “Electric Motorcycle.” Substitutes the term “electric bicycle” for the terms “bicycle with motor,” “motorized bicycles,” and similar terms. Substitutes the term “motor-driven cycle” for the term “motor scooter.” Regulates riding electric bicycles on sidewalks under certain circumstances. Prohibits on bikeways: (1) driving a vehicle, other than a bicycle, electric foot scooter, or electric micro-mobility device, with specified
exceptions; and (2) operating mopeds or electric motorcycles. Prohibits a person under the age of sixteen from operating a class 2 or class 3 electric bicycle without direct supervision. bills.raw_subject: Requires the DPR Director to establish an Important Cultural Resource Area program to allow for improved stewardship of important cultural resources in City parks. Establishes the process for including City parks in the program and requires a stewardship plan for included parks. Provides the DPR Director with rulemaking authority. Imposes fines for violations.

bills.title: RELATING TO PUBLIC PARKS, RECREATIONAL FACILITIES, AND BOTANICAL GARDENS.
bills.last_action:
See: Proposed draft version OCS2026-0182/3/30/2026 2:35 PM
Amended to CD1 and postponed to a date and time to be determined by the Committee Chair.
bills.raw_subject: Repeals certain specific fees and charges for the use of public parks and recreational facilities. Authorizes the DPR Director to establish fees and charges by rule for the commercial and noncommercial use of parks; recreational facilities; and botanical gardens under the jurisdiction of the DPR and requires that the fees and charges collected (except those derived from the use of the Hanauma Bay Nature Preserve) be deposited into a fund designated by the DPR Director. Provides the DPR Director with rulemaking authority. Establishes reporting requirements. Takes effect 450 days after approval; provided that fees established by ROH articles and sections to be repealed will remain in effect until the DPR Director adopts rules.

bills.raw_subject: Establishes a special fund to be called the Parks Maintenance and Recreation Services Revolving Fund for the deposit of all revenues and proceeds generated by the DPR relating to the use of lands dedicated for park use or for park maintenance and recreation services, or otherwise earmarked for the restoration, operation, or maintenance of beaches and parks under the jurisdiction of the DPR, to be expended for DPR operations and parks maintenance and recreational facilities under the jurisdiction of the DPR. Establishes a special fund to be called the Botanical Gardens and Urban Forestry Revolving Fund for the deposit of all revenues and proceeds relating to the use of lands dedicated for botanical gardens, community gardens, and urban forestry programs and operations generated by the DPR, or otherwise earmarked for the restoration, operation, or maintenance of parks, beaches, gardens, and street trees under the jurisdiction of the DPR, to be expended for the operations of the DPR’s Division of Urban Forestry

bills.title: RELATING TO CLEAN WATER AND NATURAL LANDS FUND.
bills.raw_subject: Establishes specific processes and procedures for evaluating proposals for expenditures from the Clean Water and Natural Lands Fund. Requires the HLM to perform preliminary due diligence on each proposal for the use of funds in the Clean Water and Natural Lands Fund. Requires a status of the land transaction report six months after Council approval! of a qualified proposal, and every six months thereafteruntil the land transaction closes.

bills.title: RELATING TO THE REAL PROPERTY TAX EXEMPTION FOR LOW-INCOME RENTAL HOUSING.
bills.raw_subject: Revises the real property tax exemption for low-income rental housing projects to apply to the assessed value of only the portion of the project's building and land attributed to the exempt floor area for dwelling units reserved for low-income households during a regulated period of at least 15 years.

bills.title: RELATING TO AFFORDABLE HOUSING INCENTIVES.
bills.last_action: Second reading notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.
bills.raw_subject: Requires the HLM to submit semi-annual reports detailing all types of incentives, along with the total amount and value of each incentive, received by every affordable housing project that has received one or more incentives from the City.

bills.title: RELATING TO AFFORDABLE HOUSING REQUIREMENTS.
bills.raw_subject: Repeals affordable housing requirement options for a developer of an IPD-T project, a PD-T project, or a TOD special district project seeking bonus height or density, or both, that required for-sale affordable housing units to remain affordable for a minimum of 30 years. Repeals the marketing period requirements for for-rental and for-sale affordable dwelling units created in compliance with ROH Chapter 29.

bills.title: RELATING TO SEWERS, DRAINAGE, AND CESSPOOLS.
bills.last_action:
CR-141(26)
Reported out for passage on third reading as amended in CD1 form.
bills.raw_subject: Requires a department to be designated by the Mayor to make certain information relating to the City's wastewater system and separate storm sewer system publicly accessible. Establishes reporting requirements.

bills.title: RELATING TO THE REGULATION OF DOGS.
bills.raw_subject: Requires the CSD Director or the head of the City agency designated by the Mayor to require the animal control contractor to enforce the provisions contained in ROH Chapter 12, Article 4. Increases existing criminal fines and establishes new civil fines.

bills.title: RELATING TO AFFORDABLE RENTAL HOUSING.
bills.raw_subject: Amends the repeal date of ROH Chapter 32, relating to affordable rental housing, to July 1, 2027. Effective July 1, 2027, repeals related provisions to reflect the proposed July 1, 2027 repeal date for ROH Chapter 32.

bills.title: RELATING TO LEASE AND RENTAL OF CITY REAL PROPERTY.
bills.raw_subject: Amends ROH Chapter 38 to address the lease and rental of City real property within the Chinatown Special District. Takes effect on July 1, 2026.

RELATING TO FUNDS.
bills.raw_subject: Abolishes the Pauahi Project Expenditures, Hawaii R-15 Fund. Upon its abolishment, the BFS Director shall transfer any remaining moneys to another appropriate fund. Takes effect on July 1, 2026.

bills.raw_subject: Amends ROH Chapter 6 to add a new article establishing the Fire Plans Review Revolving Fund. Allows moneys from the Fire Plans Review Revolving Fund to be used for certain fire prevention activities. Requires the Mayor to submit an annual report to the Council within 15 days after the Mayor submits annual budget documents.
